data.title: Market Volatility Persists Amid Tariff Concerns And Economic Data
data.description:
According to PANews, the market experienced another turbulent week as U.S. President Donald Trump pledged to impose additional tariffs, potentially diminishing the economic optimism that followed his election. Despite the volatility, gold prices continued to rise, reaching a new high above $2,950. However, questions arise about whether bullish pressure is waning as the market failed to sustain its upward momentum later in the week.

Following the release of strong U.S. CPI data, the core PCE price index will be a key focus for the market next week. Important events to watch include:

- Monday at 22:30 (UTC+8): U.S. February Dallas Fed Business Activity Index.

- Tuesday at 17:20 (UTC+8): Speech by 2026 FOMC voter and Dallas Fed President Logan.

- Wednesday at 00:45 (UTC+8): Speech by Federal Reserve Governor Barr.

- Wednesday at 02:00 (UTC+8): Speech on inflation by 2027 FOMC voter and Richmond Fed President Barkin.

- Thursday at 01:00 (UTC+8): Speech on economic outlook and housing market by 2027 FOMC voter and Atlanta Fed President Bostic.

- Thursday at 21:30 (UTC+8): U.S. Q4 GDP annualized rate revision, Q4 GDP price index revision, January durable goods orders, and weekly initial jobless claims.

- Friday at 02:15 (UTC+8): Speech on financial stability by 2026 FOMC voter and Cleveland Fed President Harker.

- Friday at 04:15 (UTC+8): Speech on economic outlook by 2026 FOMC voter and Philadelphia Fed President Harker.

- Friday at 21:30 (UTC+8): U.S. January PCE data and personal spending monthly rate.

Over the past two months, the robust U.S. economy has reassured investors to continue injecting funds into U.S. stocks despite tariff threats and Federal Reserve tightening policies. However, Friday's decline in U.S. stocks suggests that this "protection" may be limited. For investors who have recently poured record amounts into various risk investments, even initial signs of pressure on U.S. economic growth prospects are enough to trigger sell-offs. With many Federal Reserve officials speaking before the PCE data release next week, they are expected to maintain a cautious tone, emphasizing uncertainty.

data.title: Gate.io Launchpool will launch Quai Network (QUAI) mining, with an estimated annualized return of 456.25%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Gate.io Launchpool will launch Quai Network (QUAI) mining at 22:00 (UTC+8) on February 22, 2025. You can participate by staking QUAI and ETH. The total reward pool is 500,000 QUAI, with an estimated annualized return of up to 456.25%. Stake and receive, rewards are issued every hour.

data.title: CZ: Proposing to suspend Bybit withdrawals is a prudent move, Bybit CEO made the best decision based on the situation
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News CZ said about the recent Bybit hacking incident: "We have observed a pattern where hackers are able to steal large amounts of cryptocurrency from multi-signature 'cold storage' solutions, such as Bybit, Phemex, WazirX and other exchanges have encountered similar situations. In the recent Bybit case, hackers were able to make the front-end user interface show legitimate transactions, while the actual signature pointed to another transaction. For other cases, based on limited information, similar methods seem to have been used. 
More worryingly, the affected exchanges use different multi-signature solution providers. Hacker group Lazarus Group This demonstrates an extremely advanced and extensive penetration capability. It is still unclear whether the hackers were able to penetrate multiple signature devices, the server side, or both.
Some people question my previous suggestion to suspend withdrawals as a standard security precaution (I tweeted on the shuttle bus to the airport). My intention was to share a practical approach based on experience and observation, but there is no absolute right or wrong way to do this. My guiding principle is always to lean on the safe side. After any security incident, all operations should be suspended to ensure that we fully understand what happened, how the hackers penetrated the system, which devices were compromised, and triple-check to ensure that they are safe before resuming operations.
Of course, suspending withdrawals may cause more panic. In 2019, after a major $40 million hack, we suspended withdrawals for a week. When we resumed withdrawals (and deposits), deposits exceeded withdrawals. This is not to say that this approach is better, each situation is different and requires judgment. I tweeted to share what may work and to show support in a timely manner. I believe Ben made the best decision based on the information he had.
Ben Maintained transparent communication and a calm demeanor while handling this challenging situation. This is in stark contrast to other CEOs who lack transparency, such as WazirX, FTX, etc.

The cases mentioned here are all different. FTX is a fraud, and as for WazirX, I will not comment due to ongoing litigation.

The bottom line is that we should never take security for granted. It is important to understand security so that you can choose the right tools for your needs, but the basic concepts still apply. Stay safe (SAFU)! ”

data.title: Altcoin News: CryptoQuant CEO Declares Altcoin Season Has Begun, Driven by Stablecoin Holders
data.description: The next altcoin season has officially begun, but unlike past cycles, this one is not fueled by Bitcoin-to-altcoin rotations—it’s being driven by stablecoin holders, according to Ki Young Ju, CEO of CryptoQuant.In a Feb. 20 post on X, Young Ju noted that altcoin trading volume is now 2.7 times higher than Bitcoin's, signaling a shift in market dynamics. Historically, altseasons occurred when traders took profits from Bitcoin and reinvested in altcoins. However, this cycle is different.Stablecoins Fueling the Altcoin RallyInstead of Bitcoin dominance declining—traditionally a sign of an altseason—stablecoin capital is flowing into select altcoins, according to CryptoQuant’s analysis. This shift follows a sharp increase in stablecoin market capitalization, which surged after Donald Trump’s U.S. election victory. As of Feb. 21, total stablecoin market cap stands at $232 billion, per CoinGecko.However, this altseason is highly selective, Young Ju warned. Not all altcoins are rallying, and liquidity remains limited, with some networks, such as Solana, experiencing investor outflows due to rug pulls and insider schemes in the memecoin sector.Bitcoin's Institutional Adoption Alters Market TrendsMeanwhile, Bitcoin is increasingly decoupling from the broader crypto market due to institutional adoption. U.S. Bitcoin ETFs now hold over $100 billion worth of BTC, and public companies have accumulated more than $60 billion in Bitcoin as an inflation hedge, according to BitcoinTreasuries.NET.Young Ju previously highlighted that Bitcoin has effectively built its own "paper-based Layer 2 ecosystem" through ETFs, funds, and corporate treasury holdings, reducing its correlation to altcoins.As a result, only a handful of altcoins are attracting new liquidity and establishing independent price trends, marking a new era for altcoin market cycles, according to Cointelegraph.
